About Me

Aerospace Engineer
& Systems Thinker

My name is Dinuka Hannadige, and I am an aerospace engineering student based in Boulder, Colorado. I am currently pursuing a Bachelor of Science in Aerospace Engineering at the University of Colorado Boulder, with a strong interest in engineering design, CAD modeling, and hands-on problem solving.

Through coursework and competitive engineering teams, I have developed experience in the engineering design process, technical documentation, and collaborative project work. Outside of academics, I serve as a propulsion engineer for Colorado Design Build Fly, motivated to apply engineering principles to real-world challenges throughout my academic career.

03

Projects

P.01
Formula SAE
ER-08 Race Car
2023 — 2024
ERAU Motorsports
Competition Day — ER-08 on Track The ERAU Motorsports ER-08 (#106) navigating an autocross course at the FSAE competition. The aerodynamic nose cone and sidepods I helped design are clearly visible, generating downforce through the high-speed corners.
Chassis Delivery The freshly-delivered ER-08 tubular steel spaceframe (foreground) arrives in the ERAU Motorsports workshop, with the previous ER-07 visible behind it. The gold bow marks the moment the team takes ownership of the new chassis.
Bare Spaceframe — Front View A detailed frontal view of the welded tubular steel spaceframe. This critical structure serves as the mounting point for the double wishbone suspension geometry and the aerodynamic package I helped develop and validate.
Aerodynamics & Vehicle Dynamics Engineer

As a key engineering team member for ERAU Motorsports competing in the global FSAE collegiate design series, I contributed to designing, building, and racing a high-performance formula-style vehicle that balanced speed, agility, and cost-effectiveness.

  • Utilized Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) tools to design specific aero components, improving downforce and cornering capabilities of the ER-08.
  • Contributed to development of an aero rake and conducted pressure tapping tests to correlate CFD models against real-world on-track performance data.
  • Tuned the double wishbone suspension system to optimize mechanical grip and handling predictability.
  • Participated through Critical Design Review (CDR) and early shakedown testing, troubleshooting mechanical integration before competition.
P.02
Colorado Design
Build Fly (AIAA)
2025 — 2026
CU Boulder
Flight Test Day — Colorado DBF Team The full Colorado Design Build Fly team gathered at the airfield following a successful prototype flight test. Field validation sessions like this are essential for tuning our aerodynamic models and building pilot confidence before competition.
Prototype — Systems Layout Top-down view of our "Pink Foam" rapid prototype. This low-fidelity model verified the placement of dual propulsion motors, ESCs, servos, and the banner-release mechanism before committing to expensive composite materials.
Pre-Flight Integration Check Collaborating with the avionics and structures leads to verify control surface mixing, servo throws, and structural integrity of the airframe immediately before a test flight at the airfield.
Mission Verification — Aircraft in Flight The prototype successfully airborne during a validation lap. For the 2026 AIAA competition, we must demonstrate sustained flight while towing a banner and carrying a full passenger and cargo payload — this flight confirmed our propulsion system meets the required thrust-to-weight ratio.
Propulsion Engineer

Core member of CU Boulder's DBF team competing in the international AIAA Design/Build/Fly competition. The 2025–2026 RFP challenged the team to design a Banner-Towing Bush Plane with STOL, banner-deploy, and payload-carrying mission requirements.

  • Conducted static thrust testing on various motor and propeller combinations, verifying performance against thrust-to-weight ratio calculations for high-drag banner-tow missions.
  • Analyzed battery discharge curves to optimize energy density for multiple flight laps while minimizing airframe weight.
  • Collaborated with Structures to design and fabricate a custom Landing Gear Test Pad for empirical durability testing against hard landings.
  • Led propulsion avionics integration into the fuselage, ensuring proper cooling, CG placement, and reliable power distribution.
  • Hands-on rapid prototyping using foam cutting and composite techniques to build a lightweight and repairable airframe.
